message.content:
WCAG 2.2’s **Perceivable** principle includes **8 success criteria** across Guidelines **1.1–1.4**. None of the Perceivable SCs are new in WCAG 2.2; the new WCAG 2.2 criteria are all in Guidelines 2.4, 2.5, 3.2, and 3.3.[1][4]

| SC # | Official name | Level | One-line description | Typical testing mode |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| 1.1.1 | **Non-text Content** | A | Verifies that all non-text content has a text alternative or is otherwise made programmatically understandable to assistive tech. | **Mixed**: partially automatable, but requires manual review for meaningful alternatives and context. |
| 1.2.1 | **Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)** | A | Verifies that prerecorded audio-only content has a transcript and prerecorded video-only content has either an audio track or a transcript. | **Partly automatable**: tools can detect media presence, but manual checking is needed for adequacy. |
| 1.2.2 | **Captions (Prerecorded)** | A | Verifies that prerecorded synchronized media with audio includes captions. | **Partly automatable**: tools can flag missing captions, but manual testing is needed to verify correctness. |
| 1.2.3 | **Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)** | A | Verifies that prerecorded synchronized media has audio description or a full media alternative when needed. | **Manual**: determining sufficiency of description/alternative is not reliably automated. |
| 1.2.4 | **Captions (Live)** | AA | Verifies that live synchronized media with audio includes captions. | **Manual**: tool support is limited; live-caption quality and availability require human review. |
| 1.2.5 | **Audio Description (Prerecorded)** | AA | Verifies that prerecorded synchronized media includes audio description. | **Manual**: automated detection is limited; human review is usually required. |
| 1.3.1 | **Info and Relationships** | A | Verifies that structure, relationships, and meaning conveyed visually are programmatically exposed. | **Mixed**: some checks can be automated, but many semantic relationships require manual testing. |
| 1.3.2 | **Meaningful Sequence** | A | Verifies that content is presented in a logical reading order when sequence affects meaning. | **Manual**: automated tools cannot reliably judge intended reading order in all cases. |
| 1.3.3 | **Sensory Characteristics** | A | Verifies that instructions do not rely solely on shape, color, size, visual location, or sound. | **Manual**: requires understanding of instruction wording and context. |
| 1.3.4 | **Orientation** | AA | Verifies that content is not restricted to a single display orientation unless essential. | **Partly automatable**: tools can detect viewport/orientation behavior, but manual validation is needed. |
| 1.3.5 | **Identify Input Purpose** | AA | Verifies that common form fields expose their purpose programmatically so browsers/AT can assist users. | **Mixed**: some automated detection is possible, but manual confirmation of coverage and use is needed. |
| 1.4.1 | **Use of Color** | A | Verifies that color is not the only means used to convey information or action. | **Manual**: automated tools can find color-dependent patterns only imperfectly. |
| 1.4.2 | **Audio Control** | A | Verifies that audio playing automatically for more than 3 seconds can be paused, stopped, or muted. | **Partly automatable**: timing and presence of controls can be checked, but behavior usually needs manual testing. |
| 1.4.3 | **Contrast (Minimum)** | AA | Verifies that text has sufficient contrast against its background, with specified exceptions. | **Highly automatable**: contrast ratio checks are well supported by tools. |
| 1.4.4 | **Resize text** | AA | Verifies that text can be resized up to 200% without loss of content or functionality. | **Partly automatable**: tools can assist, but layout and reflow impact need manual review. |
| 1.4.5 | **Images of Text** | AA | Verifies that text is presented as real text unless the image of text is essential or exempt. | **Manual**: identifying when text is “real” versus image-based and whether exceptions apply needs review. |
| 1.4.10 | **Reflow** | AA | Verifies that content reflows without horizontal scrolling at common viewport sizes. | **Partly automatable**: tools can test viewport behavior, but many failures require manual inspection. |
| 1.4.11 | **Non-text Contrast** | AA | Verifies that essential graphical objects and UI component boundaries have sufficient contrast. | **Partly automatable**: some color-contrast checks are possible, but context and boundaries often need manual review. |
| 1.4.12 | **Text Spacing** | AA | Verifies that user-applied text spacing adjustments do not break content or functionality. | **Partly automatable**: automated tests can set spacing, but visual/functional fallout often needs manual checking. |
| 1.4.13 | **Content on Hover or Focus** | AA | Verifies that hover/focus-triggered content is dismissible, hoverable, and persistent when appropriate. | **Manual**: interaction behavior and exceptions require human testing. |

A few notes on the numbering and scope:
- WCAG 2.2 **does not add any new Perceivable success criteria**; the nine new SCs in WCAG 2.2 are in other principles, not Guidelines 1.1–1.4.[4]
- The Perceivable SCs listed above are the full set for WCAG 2.2 under Guidelines **1.1, 1.2, 1.3, and 1.4**.[1][7]
- WCAG states that all success criteria are testable, but some tests can be automated while others require human testers for part or all of the evaluation.[1]
